assumptions: Assume the intragroup dividend or the related receivable is denominated in a currency that creates foreign exchange exposure at group level.
Assume the request is to identify available hedge-accounting pathways in consolidated financial statements, subject to IFRS 9 designation, documentation and effectiveness requirements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge of an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised intragroup receivable can be considered only through the IFRS 9 exception for foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items that is not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge of a highly probable intragroup transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If the exposure is documented before the dividend is recognised as a receivable, IFRS 9 permits hedge accounting for foreign currency risk on a highly probable intragroup transaction when that risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: If the economic exposure is the group’s net investment in a foreign operation rather than the dividend receivable itself, IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 provide a separate hedge-accounting model in consolidation.

approaches.label: No qualifying consolidated hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 sets the default boundary that intragroup items generally do not qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements except for the specific foreign-currency exceptions.
